title: Setup design
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"avi28","text":"The setup design focuses on creating a custom laboratory system by using epoxy resin to assemble components such as a screwcap, pipettes, and silicon tubing. The system is reinforced to ensure an airtight seal and includes a gas bag with a luer-lock valve for controlled fluid handling. The design ensures durability and a reliable seal, making it ideal for experiments that require secure and precise fluid management.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Epoxy resin: mix two parts of component A with one part of component B. Stir thoroughly. Use alcohol ink or pigment to ensure proper mixing of the components. To shorten the pot life of the resin and enhance mixability, warm the components in a water bath.

The main vessel consists of a 500 mL GL45 pressure plus laboratory bottle. To construct the plug, drill two holes, approximately 5 mm in diameter, into two opposing cavities of the bromobutyl stopper. CAUTION Drilling is mostly done by friction, therefore hot flakes of rubber may fly around
For the short silicone tube, either use a fitting hard rubber stopper, or 3D print a mold, cast it with epoxy and plug it in.
Insert the long pipette piece through the first hole, ensuring it reaches close to the bottom of the flask and protrudes 1 cm at the top.
Cut four pieces of silicone tubing to 1, 1, 2.5, and 4 cm lengths.
Cut two 5 mL serological pipettes using a circular saw for the engraving tool to a length of 165 mm and 60 mm to yield a long and a short piece, respectively. Deburr the edges with a cutter knife.
Insert the male luer-lock adaptor into the long silicon tube and secure the luer-lock valve on it
Fix the mold with hot glue and seal all gaps. Cast the mold with epoxy and let it cure.
Draw a cast on top of the screwcap with a diameter of the screwcap and a height of 1 cm. The reinforcement for the long silicone tube is optional.
Ensure that the lower part of the bromobutyl stopper is not warped and that the pipettes stand straight, then cast it with epoxy.

The short piece should just penetrate the plug for about 2 cm and protrude 1 cm on top. Place a 1 cm long piece of silicone tubing over the lower ends as a breakage guard. The upper end gets attached to the 4 cm long silicone tube.
Screw the finished cap onto the glass bottle and check for leakage. Apply slight pressure to the bottle and submerge in water to check for the leakage.
Take a spout bag and reinforce all edges and kinks with duct tape.
3D print a female luer-lock adaptor that fits in the hole of the spout bag.
Cut the spout off.
Check for leakage.
3D print a cast around the spout and the luer-lock valve to allow easy manipulation of the valve.
Attach a luer-lock valve and use hot glue to secure the adapter into place and seal it off.
Cast with epoxy.

Add a solution of 3-bromo-1-propanol ( 2 g , 1.3 mL , 14.4 mmol , 1 eq) in acetonitrile ( 10 mL ) to a solution of AgNO 3 (silver nitrate) ( 3.67 g , 21.6 mmol , 1.5 eq) in acetonitrile ( 20 mL ) and stir at Room temperature for 24:00:00 .
Conduct the analysis of 3-nitrooxypropanol via 1 H and 13 C nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR), high-resolution mass spectrometry (HRMS) and infrared spectroscopy (IR).

To the reaction mixture, add brine ( 150 mL ) and stirred for 01:00:00 . Filter the silver salts through Celite and extract the filtrate with diethyl ether (3 × 100 mL ). Wash the organic layer with brine, dry over disodium sulfate, filter, concentrate and dry under vacuum. The product is obtained as a yellow liquid ( 0.847 g , 6.99 mmol , 49% yield).
Exploded view of the screwcap assembly.
Exploded view of the gasbag assembly.
creator: Philip Laric
